notes: unplayable with mouse; works with xbox 360 gamepad; movements have look blurry; lags here and there on ultra, but  also wasn’t lag free on low.
title: Quantum Break

notes: The game runs well, however, just like the windows counterpart suffers from performance issues. Setting my 1070 ti to high performance allowed me to reach an average frame of about 45fps at 1080p on cutsom settings ( a mix of medium and low) and dropping the resolution increased my frames significantly above 60f-ps at all times with a few drops into the 44 for a very short period. Game is very playeable and does not require any tweaks, but requires capable hardware to run at its fullest.
title: Quantum Break

notes: The game runs out of the box on DXVK especially on high resolutions. It doesn't run that fluent and mouse input is a little choppy.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Mouse has issues, so controller is recommended. Besides that issue it runs ok. Not really great performance, but it runs.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Runs right out of the box, but has annoying spikes in frame time. Performance doesn't scale much depending on settings, all high is barely different from all low.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Runs out of the box but the mouse is just horrible. Sensitivity is far to high even if you zero it out and the framerate gets very choppy.  Using a controller instead of a mouse  helps a lot.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Runs out of the box but mouse control does not work properly. No problem controlling the game with a Nintendo Switch Pro controller. I did not spot any graphical issues (like with earlier Proton versions), however the performance is still very poor. The performance does not scale with settings, minimum settings at 720p has similar performance as maximum at 1600p. Game for now is practically unplayable because of poor performance on anything but possibly the very highest end GPU's/systems.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Works perfectly in theory, no problems with mouse controls. Unfortunately barely playable due to heavy stuttering (15-30FPS for roughly a second in short but irregular intervals, especially while walking/running). Almost no performance difference between lowest and highest settings.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Finally runs with reasonable FPS(almost no stuttering) with ACO & Fsync enabled and using a controller. Live Action Videos still cause  the game to crash.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Runs great since 4.11-4/DXVK 1.3.4. Stuttering is gone now.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Performance is similar to Windows with this Proton version (using Mesa/ACO). Playable out of the box but only with a controller, mouse control is unplayable. Alt+Tab to desktop does not work (game traps the mouse pointer). Ending video allegedly does not work (but I can't verify that as I have not completed the game).
title: Quantum Break

notes: The game now runs flawlessly. The performance issues were fixed by Proton 4.11-4 / DXVK 1.3.4. No stuttering and almost constant 60 FPS on ultra settings. No problems with mouse controls.
title: Quantum Break

notes: The performance issues were fixed by Proton 4.11-4 / DXVK 1.3.4. No stuttering and almost constant 60 FPS on ultra settings. No problems with mouse controls. Unfortunately the game crashes when a live action cutscene starts.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Played for about three hours, performance is great. No graphical or audio issues from what I could see. However, mouse controls are largely borked, making it almost impossible to play with mouse and keyboard. You also cannot watch the live-action TV episode that intersperse the game - on my machine, when an episode starts I crash immediately to desktop but am able to continue to the next chapter.
title: Quantum Break

notes: Game is perfectly playable. Had no issues with frame rate, input, or audio. Everything worked fine and no modifications or configuration was necessary.
